file-lister-max.description: Lists up to 20 files that are relevant to the prompt within the given directories. Unless you know which directories are relevant, omit the directories parameter. This agent is great for finding files that could be relevant to the prompt.

{}</user_message>

Instructions:
Provide an extremely short report of the locations in the codebase that could be helpful. Focus on the files that are most relevant to the user prompt.
In your report, please give a very concise analysis that includes the full paths of files that are relevant and (extremely briefly) how they could be useful.

Do not use any further tools or spawn any further agents.

You can spawn the following agents:

- file-lister-max: Lists up to 20 files that are relevant to the prompt within the given directories. Unless you know which directories are relevant, omit the directories parameter. This agent is great for finding files that could be relevant to the prompt.

value.content:
{
  "name": "auroracraft",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "workspaces": ["client", "server"],
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "npm run dev --workspace=server & npm run dev --workspace=client",
    "dev:client": "npm run dev --workspace=client",
    "dev:server": "npm run dev --workspace=server",
    "build": "npm run build --workspace=client && npm run build --workspace=server",
    "db:generate": "npm run db:generate --workspace=server",
    "db:migrate": "npm run db:migrate --workspace=server",
    "db:seed": "npm run db:seed --workspace=server"
  }
}

{
  "name": "server",
  "private": true,
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "tsx watch src/index.ts",
    "build": "tsc",
    "start": "node dist/index.js",
    "db:generate": "drizzle-kit generate",
    "db:migrate": "tsx src/db/migrate.ts",
    "db:seed": "tsx src/db/seed.ts"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@fastify/cookie": "^11.0.2",
    "@fastify/cors": "^11.0.1",
    "@fastify/static": "^9.0.0",
    "@fastify/websocket": "^11.2.0",
    "argon2": "^0.44.0",
    "dotenv": "^16.5.0",
    "drizzle-orm": "^0.44.2",
    "fastify": "^5.8.2",
    "fastify-plugin": "^5.0.1",
    "pino-pretty": "^13.0.0",
    "postgres": "^3.4.7",
    "zod": "^3.25.67"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^24.10.1",
    "drizzle-kit": "^0.31.4",
    "tsx": "^4.19.4",
    "typescript": "~5.9.3"
  }
}

import 'dotenv/config'
import { z } from 'zod'

const envSchema = z.object({
  DATABASE_URL: z.string().url(),
  PORT: z.coerce.number().default(3000),
  HOST: z.string().default('0.0.0.0'),
  NODE_ENV: z.enum(['development', 'production', 'test']).default('development'),
  SESSION_SECRET: z.string().min(16),
  COOKIE_DOMAIN: z.string().default('localhost'),
  CLIENT_URL: z.string().url().default('http://localhost:5173'),
})

export const env = envSchema.parse(process.env)

import { Cpu } from 'lucide-react' export default function AdminAIRuntimePage() { return (

AI Runtime

Configure AI model settings and runtime parameters

AI Runtime not configured

Model configuration will be available once an AI bridge (OpenCode, Claude Code, or a local model) is connected to the platform.

Awaiting bridge connection
) }

{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Bash(git -C /workspaces/AuroraCraft log --oneline -20 2>/dev/null || echo \"No commits or not a git repo\")",
      "Bash(node --version 2>/dev/null; npm --version 2>/dev/null; psql --version 2>/dev/null; docker --version 2>/dev/null)",
      "Bash(npm create vite@latest client -- --template react-ts 2>&1)",
      "Bash(npm install 2>&1 | tail -20)",
      "Bash(npm view @fastify/cookie versions --json 2>/dev/null | tail -5)",
      "Bash(npm view @fastify/cors versions --json 2>/dev/null | tail -5)",
      "Bash(npm view fastify versions --json 2>/dev/null | tail -5)",
      "Bash(npm install 2>&1 | tail -30)",
      "Bash(sudo pg_isready 2>/dev/null || sudo pg_ctlcluster 18 main start 2>/dev/null || sudo service postgresql start 2>/dev/null; pg_isready)",
      "Bash(sudo -u postgres psql -c \"CREATE USER auroracraft WITH PASSWORD 'auroracraft' CREATEDB;\" 2>/dev/null; sudo -u postgres psql -c \"CREATE DATABASE auroracraft OWNER auroracraft;\" 2>/dev/null; echo \"DB setup done\")",
      "Bash(npm run db:generate 2>&1)",
      "Bash(npm run db:migrate 2>&1)",
      "Bash(npm run db:seed 2>&1)",
      "Bash(npm run dev:server 2>&1)",
      "Bash(sleep 4 && curl -s http://localhost:3000/api/health)",
      "Bash(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/register -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -d '{\"username\":\"testuser\",\"email\":\"test@example.com\",\"password\":\"password123\"}' -v 2>&1 | grep -E \"\\(< Set-Cookie|< HTTP|^{\\)\")",
      "Bash(curl -s -o /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/logout -b /tmp/cookies-admin.txt && echo \" \\(logout\\)\" && curl -s -w \" [%{http_code}]\" http://localhost:3000/api/auth/me -b /tmp/cookies-admin.txt)",
      "Bash(npm run dev:client 2>&1)",
      "Bash(sleep 5 && curl -s -o /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" http://localhost:5173)",
      "Bash(cd /workspaces/AuroraCraft/client && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -40)",
      "Bash(npx tsc --noEmit; echo \"EXIT: $?\")",
      "Bash(docker --version && pm2 --version 2>/dev/null || echo \"PM2 not installed\" && node --version)",
      "Bash(lsof -i :3000 2>/dev/null | grep LISTEN; lsof -i :5173 2>/dev/null | grep LISTEN; lsof -i :5432 2>/dev/null | grep LISTEN)",
      "Bash(sudo service postgresql status 2>/dev/null; pg_isready 2>/dev/null)",
      "Bash(sudo npm install -g pm2 2>&1 | tail -3)",
      "Bash(sudo service postgresql stop 2>&1; pg_isready 2>/dev/null || echo \"PostgreSQL stopped\")",
      "Bash(docker compose -f /workspaces/AuroraCraft/docker-compose.yml up -d 2>&1)",
      "Bash(sudo docker compose -f /workspaces/AuroraCraft/docker-compose.yml up -d 2>&1)",
      "Bash(sudo docker info 2>&1 | grep -i -E \"runtime|version|storage\")",
      "Bash(sudo docker rm auroracraft-db 2>/dev/null; sudo docker run -d --name auroracraft-db --restart unless-stopped -e POSTGRES_USER=auroracraft -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=auroracraft -e POSTGRES_DB=auroracraft -p 5432:5432 -v auroracraft-pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data postgres:18 2>&1)",
      "Bash(sudo docker rm -f auroracraft-db 2>/dev/null; sudo systemctl restart docker 2>/dev/null || sudo service docker restart 2>/dev/null; sleep 2; sudo docker run -d --name auroracraft-db --restart unless-stopped -e POSTGRES_USER=auroracraft -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=auroracraft -e POSTGRES_DB=auroracraft -p 5432:5432 -v auroracraft-pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data postgres:18 2>&1)",
      "Bash(sudo docker rm -f auroracraft-db 2>/dev/null; sudo containerd --version 2>&1; runc --version 2>&1; sudo docker run --runtime=runc -d --name auroracraft-db -e POSTGRES_USER=auroracraft -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=auroracraft -e POSTGRES_DB=auroracraft -p 5432:5432 postgres:18 2>&1)",
      "Bash(sudo docker rm -f auroracraft-db 2>/dev/null; sudo docker network rm auroracraft_default 2>/dev/null; echo \"Cleaned up\")",
      "Bash(sudo service postgresql start 2>&1; sleep 1; pg_isready)",
      "Bash(npm run build --workspace=client 2>&1)",
      "Bash(npm run db:migrate 2>&1 && npm run db:seed 2>&1)",
      "Bash(npm run db:migrate --workspace=server 2>&1 && npm run db:seed --workspace=server 2>&1)",
      "Bash(pm2 delete all 2>/dev/null; pm2 start /workspaces/AuroraCraft/ecosystem.config.cjs 2>&1)",
      "Bash(sleep 3 && curl -s http://localhost:3000/api/health)",
      "Bash(curl -s http://localhost:3000/ | head -5)",
      "Bash(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/login -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -d '{\"login\":\"admin\",\"password\":\"admin123\"}' -c /tmp/cookies-pm2.txt && echo \"\" && curl -s http://localhost:3000/api/auth/me -b /tmp/cookies-pm2.txt)",
      "Bash(pm2 startup 2>&1)",
      "Bash(sudo env PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/share/nvm/versions/node/v24.11.1/bin /usr/local/share/nvm/versions/node/v24.11.1/lib/node_modules/pm2/bin/pm2 startup systemd -u auroracraft --hp /home/auroracraft 2>&1)",
      "Bash(pm2 save 2>&1)",
      "Bash(pm2 status 2>&1)",
      "Bash(echo \"=== Health ===\" && curl -s http://localhost:3000/api/health && echo \"\" && echo \"=== Client HTML ===\" && curl -s http://localhost:3000/ | head -1 && echo \"\" && echo \"=== SPA Fallback ===\" && curl -s -o /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" http://localhost:3000/dashboard && echo \" \\(SPA route\\)\" && echo \"=== Auth ===\" && curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/login -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -d '{\"login\":\"admin\",\"password\":\"admin123\"}' | python3 -c \"import sys,json; d=json.load\\(sys.stdin\\); print\\(f'Logged in as: {d[\\\\\"username\\\\\"]} \\({d[\\\\\"role\\\\\"]}\\)'\\)\")",
      "Bash(sudo docker system df 2>&1)",
      "Bash(sudo docker system prune -a --volumes -f 2>&1)",
      "mcp__TestSprite__testsprite_bootstrap",
      "mcp__TestSprite__testsprite_generate_code_summary",
      "mcp__TestSprite__testsprite_generate_standardized_prd",
      "mcp__TestSprite__testsprite_generate_frontend_test_plan",
      "mcp__TestSprite__testsprite_generate_code_and_execute",
      "Bash(node /home/auroracraft/.npm/_npx/8ddf6bea01b2519d/node_modules/@testsprite/testsprite-mcp/dist/index.js generateCodeAndExecute)"
    ]
  }
}

<user_message>Find all configuration files, environment variables, package.json dependencies related to AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, or LLM integration in AuroraCraft

{}</user_message>

Instructions:
Provide an extremely short report of the locations in the codebase that could be helpful. Focus on the files that are most relevant to the user prompt.

function.name: spawn_agents

parameters.description: Spawn multiple agents and send a prompt and/or parameters to each of them. These agents will run in parallel. Note that that means they will run independently. If you need to run agents sequentially, use spawn_agents with one agent at a time instead.

prompt.description: Prompt to send to the agent

params.description: Parameters object for the agent (if any)

function.name: file-lister-max
function.description: Lists up to 20 files that are relevant to the prompt within the given directories. Unless you know which directories are relevant, omit the directories parameter. This agent is great for finding files that could be relevant to the prompt.

parameters.description: Lists up to 20 files that are relevant to the prompt within the given directories. Unless you know which directories are relevant, omit the directories parameter. This agent is great for finding files that could be relevant to the prompt.

directories.description: Optional list of paths to directories to look within. If omitted, the entire project tree is used.

messages.content:
You are an expert at finding relevant files in a codebase. # Project file tree

As Buffy, you have access to all the files in the project.

The following is the path to the project on the user's computer. It is also the current working directory for terminal commands:
<project_path>
/workspaces/AuroraCraft
</project_path>

Within this project directory, here is the file tree.
Note that the file tree:
- Is cached from the start of this conversation. Files created after the start of this conversation will not appear.
- Excludes files that are .gitignored.

The project file tree below can be ignored unless you need to know what files are in the project.

server/

content.text:
<user_message>Find all configuration files, environment variables, package.json dependencies related to AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, or LLM integration in AuroraCraft

{}</user_message>

Instructions:
Provide an extremely short report of the locations in the codebase that could be helpful. Focus on the files that are most relevant to the user prompt.
In your report, please give a very concise analysis that includes the full paths of files that are relevant and (extremely briefly) how they could be useful.

Do not use any further tools or spawn any further agents.

You can spawn the following agents:

- file-lister-max: Lists up to 20 files that are relevant to the prompt within the given directories. Unless you know which directories are relevant, omit the directories parameter. This agent is great for finding files that could be relevant to the prompt.

function.name: spawn_agents

parameters.description: Spawn multiple agents and send a prompt and/or parameters to each of them. These agents will run in parallel. Note that that means they will run independently. If you need to run agents sequentially, use spawn_agents with one agent at a time instead.

prompt.description: Prompt to send to the agent

params.description: Parameters object for the agent (if any)

function.name: file-lister-max
function.description: Lists up to 20 files that are relevant to the prompt within the given directories. Unless you know which directories are relevant, omit the directories parameter. This agent is great for finding files that could be relevant to the prompt.

parameters.description: Lists up to 20 files that are relevant to the prompt within the given directories. Unless you know which directories are relevant, omit the directories parameter. This agent is great for finding files that could be relevant to the prompt.

directories.description: Optional list of paths to directories to look within. If omitted, the entire project tree is used.
